concerns.text: Task T008c enforces a CRITICAL error and SKIPS classifier training (T009) if n < 300. This contradicts spec.md Edge Cases which mandate: 'defaulting to a simpler heuristic (e.g., fixed k=2) in such cases' and 'recommend expanding the dataset'. The task implementation blocks the pipeline entirely, violating the spec's requirement to proceed with a fallback heuristic.

Task T022a explicitly states 'Do NOT exit with code 1 if passed is false'. This violates spec.md SC-002 which requires verifying a token reduction of 'at least 30%'. By allowing the pipeline to continue and generate reports even when the success criterion fails, the task silently relaxes the requirement that the outcome be a pass/fail verification of the research hypothesis.

Task T014 validates the proxy on the 'Validation set' but fails to explicitly enforce the spec.md FR-006 requirement of a 'hold-out set of at least 20 trajectories'. Without a hard constraint in the task logic to verify n >= 20, the task risks executing on a set too small to satisfy the statistical validity required by the spec.

Task T025 mentions applying Bonferroni correction to 'all p-values' generically. Spec.md US-3 and SC-003 require applying it to the 'multiple hypothesis tests' (specifically win rate AND token usage) to control family-wise error rate. The task description lacks the explicit mandate to correct across these two distinct metric families, risking a semantic weakening of the statistical rigor.
